titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Elizabeth Tyrrell 13, October
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: William Upton Tyrrell is staying in a large dugout with papered walls. He expresses gratitude for a book and a magazine he received and requests coffee.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
13 October 17 My dear Mamy No news but just a line as
usual. We are out of the front
trenches and are now a long way
back though still living in a big
deep dug out. We have got our
valises up so are very comfortable
indeed. I did not get the apples yet
but expect them and the parcel
Minnie mentioned today. This is
the time for parcels. Where we are
now the best things to put in parcels
are cakes and sweets. There is a
canteen in a big town just behind
and we can send down there
every day for stuff. We get fish
fresh vegetable and almost any
tinned stuff we want but no
cakes or toffee. A tin of lobster is
also a very good thing and
that chicken & ham or tongue.
When I say we get tinned things
I mean fruit etc. Not danties
like chicken. Also I like cafe'au
lait very much. I always make
it for myself on a Tommy cooker.
The walls of this dug out are
covered with pictures out of all the
Illustrated papers and is quite
showy. No news at all
I will write to Hunter very soon
The weather is alternately very
wet and then very fine. Thanks very much for the book
and magazine. Just what I did
want badly. Best love from
your most affec Son
Willie.
